title: Viral Title Generator
prompt:
Act as a world-class YouTube strategist who has helped 100+ channels reach 1M+ subscribers. Your task is to generate 10 irresistible YouTube video titles for the following topic: [YOUR TOPIC HERE].

Requirements for each title:
• Must trigger curiosity or strong emotion (fear, excitement, FOMO)
• Include a power word (Secret, Brutal, Hidden, Exposed, Finally)
• Optimal length: 50–70 characters
• At least 3 titles should use the "Number + Noun" formula (e.g., "7 Brutal Truths")
• At least 2 should use a "How I" or "Why I" personal story hook
• 1 title must include a controversy or contrarian angle

Also provide: A 1-line explanation of WHY each title works psychologically.

My niche: [YOUR NICHE — e.g., Finance, Tech, Fitness]
Target audience: [DESCRIBE YOUR AUDIENCE — e.g., Beginners aged 18–35]
Video goal: [VIEWS / SUBS / SALES / AWARENESS]
usageSteps: Replace [YOUR TOPIC HERE] with the exact subject of your video (e.g., 'How to invest $100 in crypto').
Fill in [YOUR NICHE] — be specific. Instead of 'Finance', write 'Personal Finance for Pakistani Millennials'.
Define your [TARGET AUDIENCE] with age, experience level, and pain points.
Set your [VIDEO GOAL] — this changes the psychological hook the AI uses.
Run the prompt in ChatGPT-4 or Claude. Pick the top 3 titles and A/B test them using TubeBuddy or vidIQ.
Pro Tip: Paste the winning title back and ask: 'Create 5 thumbnail text variations for this title.'

title: Midjourney Thumbnail Creator
prompt:
/imagine prompt: YouTube thumbnail, [SUBJECT DESCRIPTION — e.g., a man in his 30s, shocked expression, pointing right], dramatic studio lighting, bold red and yellow color palette, high contrast, cinematic composition, shallow depth of field, 4K hyperrealistic photography, clean background on the right side for text overlay, rule-of-thirds framing, Canon EOS R5 style, editorial magazine quality --ar 16:9 --v 6 --style raw --q 2

Variation for illustrated style:
/imagine prompt: YouTube thumbnail illustration, [SUBJECT], flat vector art style, bold outlines, vibrant neon colors (#FF0000, #FFD700, #0A0A0A), strong shadow, pop art influence, simple clean background, text-safe zone on left 40%, professional social media design --ar 16:9 --v 6

Key Variables to Replace:
• [SUBJECT DESCRIPTION]: Describe the main person/object in detail
• Color Palette: Swap red/yellow to match your brand colors
• Style: Change 'hyperrealistic' to 'cinematic', 'anime', or 'illustration'
usageSteps: Open Midjourney Discord or midjourney.com and go to any bot channel.
Replace [SUBJECT DESCRIPTION] with a clear, visual description of your thumbnail's main element.
Adjust the color palette to match your channel's brand colors for consistency.
Use --v 6 for most photorealistic results. Use --style raw for less 'AI-looking' output.
Generate 4 variations (default) then use 'V' buttons to create upscaled sub-variations.
Pro Tip: After generating, paste the image into Canva and add your channel's text overlay using the same font as your brand kit.

title: SEO Description Optimizer
prompt:
Act as a YouTube SEO expert with 10+ years of experience ranking videos on page 1. Write a fully optimized YouTube video description for the following:

Video Title: [YOUR EXACT VIDEO TITLE]
Main Topic: [WHAT IS THE VIDEO ABOUT — 2 sentences]
Primary Keyword: [MAIN KEYWORD TO RANK FOR]
Secondary Keywords: [3-5 RELATED KEYWORDS]
Channel Niche: [YOUR NICHE]
Target Country/Language: [e.g., Pakistan, English]

Description Requirements:
• First 2 lines must be compelling (visible without 'Show more') and contain the primary keyword naturally
• Total length: 300-500 words
• Include a timestamped chapter structure (00:00 Intro, 02:30 Main Topic, etc.)
• Add 3-5 relevant hashtags at the bottom
• Include a soft CTA for subscribers and a link placeholder [YOUR LINK]
• Sprinkle secondary keywords naturally — no keyword stuffing
• End with 3 lines of social proof or channel description
usageSteps: Start with your exact video title — copy it precisely from your YouTube draft.
Research your [PRIMARY KEYWORD] using VidIQ, TubeBuddy, or Google Trends before running this prompt.
List 3-5 [SECONDARY KEYWORDS] — these should be related search terms your audience uses.
For [TARGET COUNTRY], specify even if English — YouTube uses geo-signals for recommendations.
Paste the generated description into YouTube Studio and check the keyword density using a free SEO checker.
Pro Tip: Run this prompt again after your video hits 1,000 views and update the description with new trending keywords.

title: Community Post Calendar
prompt:
You are a YouTube community engagement specialist. Generate a 30-day Community Post content calendar for my YouTube channel.

Channel Niche: [YOUR NICHE]
Channel Size: [SMALL <10K / MEDIUM 10K-100K / LARGE 100K+]
Posting Goal: [INCREASE ENGAGEMENT / GROW SUBSCRIBERS / PROMOTE VIDEOS / BUILD BRAND]
Audience Location: [PRIMARY AUDIENCE COUNTRY]

For each post, provide:
1. Post Type (Poll / Text / Image / Video Teaser / Quiz)
2. Post Copy (complete, ready-to-publish text under 300 chars)
3. Engagement Hook (the question or action that drives comments)
4. Best Time to Post (Day + Hour)
5. Expected Outcome (what this post achieves strategically)

Mix of post types:
• 10 Polls (audience research disguised as entertainment)
• 8 Behind-the-scenes text posts
• 6 Video teasers with curiosity hooks
• 4 'Hot take' opinion posts to spark debate
• 2 Community milestones / celebration posts
usageSteps: Set your [CHANNEL NICHE] — be hyper-specific. 'Tech Reviews Pakistan' is better than just 'Tech'.
Choose your [CHANNEL SIZE] honestly — this changes the tone and strategy the AI uses.
For [POSTING GOAL], pick ONE primary goal per month. Mixing goals dilutes results.
Run the prompt in Claude 3 Opus for best calendar structure, or ChatGPT-4 for more creative copy.
Schedule posts using YouTube Studio's 'Community' tab — post at the AI-suggested times.
Pro Tip: Use poll results as real data to inform your NEXT video topic — it's built-in audience research.
